diagrams.title: Patricia Tree vs Standard Trie Comparison
diagrams.description: Comparison showing how a standard trie representing keys 'A', 'to', 'tea', 'ten', 'ted' differs from its Patricia tree equivalent
elements: Standard Trie: Multiple single-child nodes visible (t→o→e→a, t→e→n, t→e→d)
Patricia Tree: Merges single-child paths into single edges
Shows compression from 9 nodes in trie to 6 nodes in Patricia
Key insight: Both support same operations but Patricia uses less memory
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how path compression reduces node count in Patricia trees compared to standard tries

diagrams.title: Ethereum Merkle Patricia Tree Node Types
diagrams.description: Four node types used in Ethereum's MPT with their structures
elements: Empty node: Just a placeholder (null/empty string)
Leaf node: [key ending, value] - stores actual data
Extension node: [shared prefix, hash of child] - compression helper
Branch node: [16 children array, optional value] - path branching point
diagrams.purpose: Understanding MPT node structure is crucial for implementing or analyzing Ethereum state management

diagrams.title: MPT Root Hash Update Process
diagrams.description: Shows how changing one value affects the root hash through the entire tree
elements: Original tree with root hash ABCD...
Only nodes along path from leaf to root need re-hashing
New hashes: leaf → branch → extension → NEW ROOT
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrate how MPT provides integrity verification through hash propagation
